pullRequest.body: This PR adds change detection functionality that builds on the [torchange](https://github.com/Z-Zheng/pytorch-change-models) package developed by @Z-Zheng, making it easier to apply AnyChnage on remote sensing imagery and visualize the results. Fix #49

pullRequest.title: Add memory-efficient tiled inference with spline blending and D4 TTA
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Implements a generic, memory-efficient tiled inference pipeline for GeoTIFF rasters as a new `geoai/inference.py` module, addressing issue #87.

- **Windowed rasterio I/O** - reads tiles directly via rasterio windows, avoiding full-image input memory allocation
- **Four blending strategies** via `BlendMode` enum: `none`, `linear`, `cosine`, and `spline` (new, from issue author's approach using `scipy.signal.windows.triang`)
- **D4 test-time augmentation** - optional 8-fold augmentation (identity, 3 rotations, h-flip, v-flip, 2 diagonal flips) for improved predictions at 8x compute cost
- **Configurable pre/post-processing hooks** - `preprocess_fn` and `postprocess_fn` callables for model-specific normalization
- **GeoTIFF metadata preservation** - CRS, transform, and nodata are carried through to output

### Key fixes vs prior attempt (PR #502)
- Dedicated `geoai/inference.py` module instead of adding to the already-large `utils.py`
- Proper boundary tile handling (crop predictions to actual tile dimensions, preventing shape mismatch)
- Input validation (`overlap >= tile_size` raises `ValueError`)
- `num_classes` parameter actually used to size the output accumulator
- No redundant device transfers
- Pure `torch.rot90`/`torch.flip` for D4 transforms (no `torchvision` dependency)

### Files changed
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `geoai/inference.py` | New module: `BlendMode`, `create_weight_mask`, `d4_forward`, `d4_inverse`, `d4_tta_forward`, `predict_geotiff` |
| `geoai/__init__.py` | Lazy import registration for all public symbols |
| `tests/test_inference.py` | 35 tests: imports, signatures, weight mask behavior, D4 roundtrips, validation |
| `docs/inference.md` | mkdocstrings API doc page |
| `mkdocs.yml` | Nav entry for inference module |

## Usage

```python
from geoai.inference import predict_geotiff

predict_geotiff(
    model=my_model,
    input_raster="input.tif",
    output_raster="output.tif",
    tile_size=256,
    overlap=64,
    blend_mode="spline",
    tta=False,
)
```

## Test plan

- [x] All 35 unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_inference.py -v`)
- [x] Lazy imports verified (`from geoai import predict_geotiff, BlendMode`)
- [x] Weight mask symmetry, shape, dtype, and value range validated for all 4 modes
- [x] D4 roundtrip verified (forward + inverse = identity for all 8 transforms)
- [x] Input validation tested (FileNotFoundError, ValueError for invalid overlap)

Closes #87

## References
- Spline blending: https://github.com/Vooban/Smoothly-Blend-Image-Patches
- Original issue with source code: #87

pullRequest.title: Add Flip-n-Slide data augmentation strategy
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Implements the **Flip-n-Slide** data augmentation strategy for geospatial imagery tiling, based on the paper:

> Abrahams, E., Snow, T., Siegfried, M. R., & Pérez, F. (2024). *A Concise Tiling Strategy for Preserving Spatial Context in Earth Observation Imagery*. ML4RS Workshop @ ICLR 2024. [arXiv:2404.10927](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.10927)

Closes #88

## What's new

### New functions in `geoai/utils.py`

- **`flipnslide_augmentation(image, tile_size=256, ...)`** — Core algorithm that accepts a numpy array (channels, height, width) or raster file path and produces overlapping tiles with 8 distinct augmentations (4 rotations + 4 flip/rotation combos). Returns tiles as numpy array or PyTorch tensor.

- **`export_flipnslide_tiles(in_raster, out_folder, ...)`** — Geospatial-aware wrapper that reads rasters with rasterio, applies FlipnSlide tiling to both image and optional mask simultaneously, and exports each tile as an individual GeoTIFF with proper CRS and geotransform.

### Updated functions

- **`export_geotiff_tiles(..., tiling_strategy='flipnslide')`** — Added `tiling_strategy` parameter with options `'grid'` (default) and `'flipnslide'`.

### New notebook

- **`docs/examples/flipnslide_augmentation.ipynb`** — Demo notebook showing all three usage patterns with visualizations.

## Algorithm

The FlipnSlide algorithm generates two sets of overlapping tiles:

1. **Standard tiles** at half-stride with rotational augmentations (identity, 90°, 180°, 270°)
2. **Inner offset tiles** (inset by tile_size/2 from edges) with flip+rotation augmentations (hflip, vflip, 90°+hflip, 90°+vflip)

This produces diverse training samples while preserving spatial context — every overlapping pixel pair receives a different transformation, eliminating redundancy.

## Implementation notes

- Pure numpy/torch implementation — no tensorflow or flipnslide package dependency
- Faithfully follows the original `sliding_transforms` algorithm from the [flipnslide](https://github.com/elliesch/flipnslide) package
- Both new functions are exported in `__init__.py`

---
Closes #88

pullRequest.title: Add DeepForest segmentation panel to QGIS plugin
pullRequest.body: ## Summary Add a new DeepForest dock widget to the GeoAI QGIS plugin for object detection in remote sensing imagery using pretrained deep learning models. ## New Files - `qgis_plugin/geoai_plugin/dialogs/deepforest_panel.py` — DeepForest dock widget (1284 lines) ## Modified Files - `qgis_plugin/geoai_plugin/geoai_plugin.py` — Wire up toolbar action, GPU cleanup, about dialog ## Features ### Model Tab - 5 pretrained HuggingFace models: tree crown, bird, livestock, nest, dead tree detection - Configurable model revision - Device selection (auto/cuda/cpu) with CUDA auto-detection - Async model loading via QThread worker - Input from QGIS raster layer or file (including GeoPackage rasters) ### Predict Tab - **Single Image** mode using `predict_image()` - **Large Tile** mode using `predict_tile()` with configurable: - Patch size (64–2048) - Patch overlap (0.0–0.9) - IoU threshold (0.0–1.0) - Dataloader strategy (single/batch/window) - Score threshold filtering ### Output Tab - Save as vector: GeoPackage, Shapefile, GeoJSON - Save as raster: GeoTIFF (rasterized bounding boxes) - Export training data in COCO and YOLO formats - Auto-add results to QGIS map ## Dependencies - `deepforest>=2.0.0`

pullRequest.title: Add CLIP-based zero-shot classification for vector features
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds `CLIPVectorClassifier` class and `clip_classify_vector()` convenience function for zero-shot CLIP classification of vector polygon features
- For each polygon, extracts the bounding-box image chip from a raster, encodes it with CLIP, and assigns the best-matching category from a user-provided label list via cosine similarity
- Registers new symbols in `__init__.py` lazy loading infrastructure
- Includes 32 unit tests covering imports, signatures, validation, lazy registration, RGB conversion, and chip extraction

## Features

- **Zero-shot classification**: No training data required — just provide candidate labels
- **Flexible inputs**: Accepts `GeoDataFrame` or file paths for vector data; any raster format supported by rasterio
- **Performance optimized**: Pre-computes text embeddings once, processes image chips in configurable batches
- **Automatic CRS alignment**: Reprojects vector data to match raster CRS when they differ
- **Edge case handling**: Empty/null geometries, out-of-bounds polygons, tiny chips, multi-band/single-band/float rasters
- **Output formats**: Save results as `.geojson`, `.parquet`, or `.gpkg`

## Usage

```python
from geoai import clip_classify_vector

result = clip_classify_vector(
    "buildings.geojson",
    "naip_image.tif",
    labels=["residential", "commercial", "industrial", "park"],
)
print(result[["clip_label", "clip_confidence"]].head())
```

## Test plan

- [x] 32 unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_clip_classify.py -v`)
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (black, codespell, trailing whitespace)
- [x] Lazy import infrastructure tests pass (`pytest tests/test_lazy_import.py -v`)
- [x] Tests use mocked models — no CLIP download required in CI

Closes #129

pullRequest.title: Add Super-Resolution Module for GeoAI
pullRequest.body: Dear Prof. Wu, I have implemented a new super-resolution module for multispectral images in the GeoAI project. The module leverages the OpenSR latent diffusion model to enhance image resolution and generate corresponding uncertainty maps. I have committed the changes to my fork and would like to request merging them into the main repository. jupyter example: import geoai import os os.environ["http_proxy"] = "http://127.0.0.1:10810" os.environ["https_proxy"] = "http://127.0.0.1:10810" lr_path = '2024.tif' sr_path = '2024_sr.tif' uncertainty_path = '2024_uncertainty.tif' sr_image,uncertainty = geoai.super_resolution(input_lr_path =lr_path, output_sr_path=sr_path, output_uncertainty_path=uncertainty_path,rgb_nir_bands = [3,2,1,4]) ax = geoai.view_image(sr_path) ax = geoai.view_image(lr_path,bdx = [2,1,0]) ax = geoai.view_image(uncertainty_path)

pullRequest.title: Add ChangeStar building change detection support
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Adds support for ChangeStar building change detection models from the [torchange](https://github.com/Z-Zheng/pytorch-change-models) package (Changen2 pre-trained weights).

## Changes

### `geoai/change_detection.py`
- **`ChangeStarDetection` class**: Wraps torchange's ChangeStar models with:
  - GeoTIFF I/O with proper CRS and transform handling
  - Support for all 7 pretrained ChangeStar variants (s0/s1/s9, vitb/vitl backbones)
  - Tiled processing for large rasters with configurable tile size and overlap
  - Binary change map, change probability, and semantic segmentation (T1/T2) outputs
  - Vector output (GeoJSON, GPKG, Shapefile) via rasterio.features.shapes
  - Built-in visualization methods (`visualize()`, `visualize_overlay()`)
- **`changestar_detect()`**: Convenience function for one-step usage
- **`list_changestar_models()`**: Lists available model variants

### `geoai/__init__.py`
- Registers `ChangeStarDetection`, `changestar_detect`, and `list_changestar_models` with try/except guard

### `docs/examples/changestar.ipynb`
- Notebook demonstrating: model listing, initialization, prediction, visualization (standard + overlay), model comparison (S1 vs S9), threshold adjustment, and output file inspection

### `mkdocs.yml`
- Added notebook to navigation

## Dependencies
- `torchange` (already in `pyproject.toml` extra deps)
- `albumentations` (required for preprocessing)
- `ever-beta` (auto-installed with torchange)

Closes #215

pullRequest.title: Add impervious surface mapping example notebook
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds a new example notebook (`docs/examples/impervious_surface_mapping.ipynb`) for impervious surface mapping using NAIP aerial imagery and DC Open Data, resolving #225
- Covers the full ML workflow: data download, tile generation, U-Net training (ResNet34 encoder), inference, vectorization, geometric analysis, and interactive visualization
- Includes a data preparation script (`scripts/prepare_dc_impervious_data.py`) that downloads NAIP from Planetary Computer and impervious surface polygons from DC GIS REST API

## Data Requirements

The notebook references two files on HuggingFace that need to be uploaded to `giswqs/geospatial`:
- `dc_naip.tif` - 3-band RGB NAIP imagery (18.6 MB, 2711×2954 px, EPSG:26918, 2023-11-13), clipped to central DC (National Mall area)
- `dc_impervious_surfaces.geojson` - 1,656 impervious surface polygons (8.2 MB, EPSG:26918) from [DC Impervious Surface 2023](https://opendata.dc.gov/datasets/DCGIS::impervious-surface-2023)

**To generate these files**, run:
```bash
pip install planetary-computer pystac-client
python scripts/prepare_dc_impervious_data.py
```
This downloads NAIP from Planetary Computer and polygons from the DC GIS REST API, clips both to the target bbox `(-77.045, 38.885, -77.025, 38.900)`, and saves them to `docs/examples/`.

## Test Results (Google Colab, T4 GPU)

| Step | Result |
|------|--------|
| Tile export | 110 tiles (512×512), 100% with features |
| Training | 20 epochs, best Val IoU: **0.7234** (epoch 18) |
| Inference | 3.26s, 2 classes, 58.7% impervious |
| Vectorization | 116 polygons, all > 50 m² |
| Visualization | All interactive maps rendered |

## Test plan
- [x] Upload `dc_naip.tif` and `dc_impervious_surfaces.geojson` to HuggingFace `giswqs/geospatial`
- [x] Run full notebook end-to-end on Google Colab
- [x] Verify all visualization cells render correctly
- [x] Confirm mkdocs documentation builds

Closes #225

pullRequest.title: Add DINOv3 finetuning for image segmentation
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #250

Adds support for finetuning DINOv3 (Vision Transformer) backbones for semantic segmentation of geospatial imagery.

### Key components

- **`DINOv3Segmenter`** - PyTorch Lightning module with frozen DINOv3 backbone, multi-scale feature extraction from intermediate ViT layers, and a DPT (Dense Prediction Transformer) decoder head
- **`DINOv3SegmentationDataset`** - GeoTIFF-aware dataset with rasterio loading, configurable input channels, and nodata-aware mask padding (`ignore_index=255`)
- **`train_dinov3_segmentation()`** - End-to-end training with train/val/test splits, early stopping, model checkpointing, and CosineAnnealing LR scheduler
- **`dinov3_segment_geotiff()`** - Sliding-window inference on large GeoTIFFs with batched GPU processing, overlap blending via probability-based soft voting, and CRS/transform preservation

### Internal components (not exported in public API)

- **`LoRALinear`** - Low-Rank Adaptation wrapper for parameter-efficient backbone finetuning
- **`DPTSegmentationHead`** - Multi-scale feature fusion with progressive upsampling for dense per-pixel prediction

### Architecture decisions

- **DPT decoder** (proven for dense prediction with ViTs) over linear probes or FPN
- **Multi-scale extraction** from 4 evenly-spaced intermediate ViT layers using specific indices (e.g. `[2, 5, 8, 11]` for ViT-S/B) rather than last-N layers
- **LoRA adaptation** for memory-efficient backbone tuning (only trains low-rank A/B matrices)
- Supports all DINOv3 model sizes (S/B/L/g) with automatic extraction layer selection based on transformer depth
- Follows existing project patterns from `timm_segment.py` for Lightning integration and lazy imports
- Uses `get_device()` from `geoai.utils.device` (consistent with `clip_classify.py`) instead of a duplicated private helper
- Validates `output_dir` parent directory exists before training

### Files changed

- `geoai/dinov3_finetune.py` - New module with `__all__` exporting only user-facing API
- `geoai/__init__.py` - Added lazy imports for 4 public symbols (`DINOv3Segmenter`, `DINOv3SegmentationDataset`, `train_dinov3_segmentation`, `dinov3_segment_geotiff`) + submodule
- `tests/test_dinov3_finetune.py` - 52 tests (import, export, signature, unit, edge cases)
- `docs/examples/dinov3_finetune_segmentation.ipynb` - Notebook example with Colab outputs (data download, chip creation, training, inference, vectorization, visualization)
- `mkdocs.yml` - Added notebook to docs navigation

## Test plan

- [x] All 52 tests pass locally (51 passed, 1 skipped without Lightning installed)
- [x] LoRALinear: output shape, frozen original, trainable A/B, zero-init B, alpha defaults
- [x] DPTSegmentationHead: output shapes for various configs and spatial sizes
- [x] DINOv3Segmenter (mocked backbone): init, forward shape, frozen backbone, trainable decoder, loss config, mIoU computation with ignore_index
- [x] Overlap validation: `overlap >= window_size` raises ValueError
- [x] Dataset: mismatched lengths, empty paths raise ValueError
- [x] Lazy imports resolve correctly from `geoai` namespace
- [x] Notebook runs end-to-end on Google Colab (NVIDIA L4): val_loss 0.736 → 0.277 over 20 epochs, 712 building features detected

pullRequest.title: Add TESSERA support
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Adds support for [TESSERA](https://github.com/ucam-eo/tessera) (Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis) embeddings via the [geotessera](https://github.com/ucam-eo/geotessera) library.

Closes #271

## Changes

- **`geoai/tessera.py`** — New module with convenience functions wrapping the geotessera library:
  - `tessera_download()` — Download embeddings as GeoTIFF or NumPy arrays
  - `tessera_fetch_embeddings()` — Fetch embeddings directly into memory
  - `tessera_coverage()` — Generate coverage maps showing data availability
  - `tessera_visualize_rgb()` — Create false-color RGB composites from embedding bands
  - `tessera_tile_count()` — Check number of available tiles in a region
  - `tessera_available_years()` — List years with available embeddings
  - `tessera_sample_points()` — Extract embedding vectors at point locations for downstream ML tasks

- **`geoai/__init__.py`** — Expose TESSERA functions at package level (optional import, graceful fallback)
- **`docs/tessera.md`** — API reference documentation
- **`docs/examples/tessera.ipynb`** — Example notebook demonstrating all features
- **`mkdocs.yml`** — Added tessera notebook and API reference entries

## Dependencies

- `geotessera` (optional, installed separately via `pip install geotessera` or `conda install -c conda-forge geotessera`)

pullRequest.title: Fix multi-class segmentation mask handling in segmentation.py
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #335 — `geoai/segmentation.py` was hardcoded for binary segmentation only, while `train.py` already received multi-class support in PR #503. This PR aligns `segmentation.py` with that fix.

### Bug fixes
- **Binary threshold bug**: Replaced `mask > 127` with a class-aware `_normalize_mask` helper that handles both binary (any nonzero -> 1) and multi-class (clip to valid range) masks
- **Tensor conversion crash**: `mask.clone.detach.long` failed when mask was a numpy array; now uses `torch.as_tensor` with a type check
- **Missing `num_classes` parameter**: Added to `prepare_datasets` and `train_model` with default `2` for backward compatibility
- **SegFormer misconfiguration**: Model now loads with `num_labels=num_classes` and `ignore_mismatched_sizes=True`
- **Visualization overflow**: Multi-class masks are scaled evenly across 0-255 with `tab20` colormap instead of binary `* 255` with grayscale

### Tests
- 9 new CPU-only tests (no torch/GPU required) covering `_normalize_mask` logic and visualization scaling
- All existing tests unaffected

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest tests/test_segmentation.py -v` — 9 passed, 11 skipped (skips are pre-existing, require torch)
- [ ] Maintainer review of mask normalization logic vs `train.py` reference implementation

pullRequest.title: Fix NameError when torchange not installed
pullRequest.body:
The import error handler for the optional `torchange` dependency didn't define the imported names, causing `NameError: name 'AnyChange' is not defined` when instantiating `ChangeDetection`. ## Changes - Set `AnyChange` and `show_change_masks` to `None` in the import `except` block - Add runtime checks in `_init_model()` and `visualize_changes()` that raise `ImportError` with installation instructions when these are `None` ```python try: from torchange.models.segment_any_change import AnyChange, show_change_masks except ImportError: AnyChange = None show_change_masks = None ``` The module now imports successfully without `torchange` and provides actionable error messages only when attempting to use the functionality.
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > NameError: name 'AnyChange' is not defined > > > > NameError Traceback (most recent call last) > [/tmp/ipython-input-255165693.py](https://localhost:8080/#) in () > 5 > 6 # # # Initialize change detection > ----> 7 detector = ChangeDetection(sam_model_type="vit_h") > 8 > 9 # # Configure parameters (following the torchange example) > > 1 frames > [/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/geoai/change_detection.py](https://localhost:8080/#) in _init_model(self) > 40 self.sam_checkpoint = download_checkpoint(self.sam_model_type) > 41 > ---> 42 self.model = AnyChange(self.sam_model_type, sam_checkpoint=self.sam_checkpoint) > 43 > 44 # Set default hyperparameters > > NameError: name 'AnyChange' is not defined > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >
- Fixes opengeos/geoai#340 --- 💬 We'd love your input! Share your thoughts on Copilot coding agent in our [2 minute survey](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-survey).

pullRequest.title: Add support for custom transform
pullRequest.body:
Fix #342 #339

```python
from geoai.train import (
      train_segmentation_model,
      SemanticTransforms,
      SemanticRandomHorizontalFlip,
      SemanticRandomVerticalFlip,
      SemanticRandomRotation90,
      SemanticBrightnessAdjustment,
  )

  # Create custom augmentation pipeline
  train_transforms = SemanticTransforms([
      SemanticRandomHorizontalFlip(prob=0.5),
      SemanticRandomVerticalFlip(prob=0.5),
      SemanticRandomRotation90(prob=0.5),
      SemanticBrightnessAdjustment(brightness_range=(0.8, 1.2), prob=0.5),
  ])

  # Train model with augmentation
  model = train_segmentation_model(
      images_dir="path/to/images",
      labels_dir="path/to/labels",
      output_dir="path/to/output",
      train_transforms=train_transforms,  # Custom augmentation
      # ... other parameters
  )
```

pullRequest.title: Disable SamGeo 1 and 2 due to compatibility issues
Implement sliding window method for Moondream VLM to handle large images
pullRequest.body:
## Implement sliding window method for Moondream VLM

Fixes #417

Added sliding window processing to handle large images that Moondream VLM struggles with. Images are divided into overlapping tiles, processed independently, and results are merged.

### Changes
- **New Methods**: `detect_sliding_window()`, `point_sliding_window()`, `query_sliding_window()`, `caption_sliding_window()`
- **NMS Implementation**: Non-Maximum Suppression to merge overlapping detections from adjacent tiles
- **Convenience Functions**: Simple one-line functions for quick usage
- **Example Notebook**: Complete tutorial with usage examples
- **Unit Tests**: Test coverage for new functionality

### Features
- Configurable window size and overlap
- Progress bar support
- Two combine strategies: concatenate & summarize
- Automatic fallback to regular methods for small images
- Preserves georeferencing for GeoTIFF outputs

### Usage
```python
from geoai import MoondreamGeo

processor = MoondreamGeo()
result = processor.detect_sliding_window(
    large_image, 
    "car", 
    window_size=512, 
    overlap=64
)

pullRequest.title: Fix: Add PyQt dependency for Windows pixi installation
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #466

## Problem
Windows users installing QGIS via pixi were encountering:
```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'PyQt5'
```

This occurred because PyQt5 was not being installed as a dependency, even though QGIS requires it.

## Solution
Added `pyqt = "5.15.*"` to all pixi.toml example configurations in the documentation:
- CUDA 12.x
- CUDA 13.x  
- CPU

## Changes
- Updated `docs/qgis_plugin.md` with PyQt dependency
- Updated `qgis_plugin/README.md` with PyQt dependency

This ensures PyQt5 is installed alongside QGIS, resolving the import error on Windows.

pullRequest.title: Fix: Export orthogonalize function in package
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #467

The QGIS plugin calls `geoai.orthogonalize()` but the function wasn't exported in the package's `__init__.py`. This PR adds the missing import.

**Changes:**
- Added `orthogonalize`, `regularization`, `hybrid_regularization`, and `adaptive_regularization` to the package exports in `geoai/__init__.py`

This makes the functions accessible when the geoai package is imported, resolving the `module 'geoai_external' has no attribute 'orthogonalize'` error.

pullRequest.title: Add support for exporting data in COCO and YOLO formats
pullRequest.body: Fix #487

pullRequest.title: Update paper.md to use backticks for software names
pullRequest.body: Fix #496

pullRequest.title: Fix dependency conflicts and code quality improvements
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Resolves #504 — dependency conflict between `deepforest` (numpy<2) and `buildingregulariser` (numpy>=2.0.0).

## Changes

### Dependency cleanup (`requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml`)

1. **Move `buildingregulariser` from hard to optional dependency** (fixes #504)
   - The conflict: `deepforest` (conda 2.0.0) requires `numpy<2`, while `buildingregulariser` requires `numpy>=2.0.0`. Having `buildingregulariser` as a hard dependency makes it impossible to install geoai alongside deepforest.
   - The code in `utils.py` already handles the absence of `buildingregulariser` gracefully via `try/except ImportError` with an `install_package()` fallback, so it was effectively already optional.
   - Added a new `building` optional dependency group: `pip install geoai-py[building]`
   - Users who need building regularization can install it explicitly; users who need deepforest compatibility are no longer blocked.

2. **Remove unused `ever-beta`** — This package (EVer library for Earth Vision) is not imported anywhere in the geoai codebase. It was a dependency of `torchange`, which is already commented out in requirements.txt.

3. **Remove unused `psutil`** — Not imported anywhere in the geoai Python source code.

### Code quality: Replace bare `except:` with `except Exception:`

Fixed 13 bare `except:` clauses across 5 files:
- `geoai/extract.py` (6 occurrences)
- `geoai/hf.py` (3 occurrences)
- `geoai/moondream.py` (2 occurrences)
- `geoai/prithvi.py` (1 occurrence)
- `geoai/train.py` (1 occurrence)

Bare `except:` catches `SystemExit`, `KeyboardInterrupt`, and `GeneratorExit` in addition to regular exceptions, which can mask critical errors and make debugging difficult. Using `except Exception:` is the Python best practice (PEP 8, B001) for catch-all error handling.

## No breaking changes

- `buildingregulariser` was already lazily imported with a fallback — existing code that calls `regularization()` will still work (it auto-installs the package if missing).
- The `except Exception:` change preserves identical behavior for all normal error cases.

pullRequest.title: Add ONNX Runtime support for geospatial model inference
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Adds ONNX Runtime support for geospatial model inference, enabling deployment on edge devices and environments without GPU drivers.

Closes #506

## Changes

### New module: `geoai/onnx.py`

- **`export_to_onnx()`** — Convert PyTorch/HuggingFace models to ONNX format with automatic metadata sidecar (`.onnx.json`) containing task, label mappings, and input dimensions
- **`ONNXGeoModel`** — Load and run inference with ONNX models using ONNX Runtime, with full GeoTIFF I/O support:
  - Semantic segmentation (with tiled processing for large rasters)
  - Image classification
  - Object detection
  - Depth estimation
  - Mask-to-vector conversion via `mask_to_vector()`
  - GeoTIFF output with preserved CRS/transform
  - API mirrors `AutoGeoModel` (`predict()`, `mask_to_vector()`, etc.)
- **`onnx_semantic_segmentation()`** — Convenience function for segmentation
- **`onnx_image_classification()`** — Convenience function for classification

### Other changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added `onnx` optional dependency group (`pip install geoai-py[onnx]`)
- **`__init__.py`**: Registered ONNX exports with `try/except` for optional import
- **`docs/onnx.md`**: API reference page
- **`mkdocs.yml`**: Added onnx module to nav

## Usage

```python
from geoai import export_to_onnx, ONNXGeoModel

# Export a HuggingFace model to ONNX
export_to_onnx(
    "nvidia/segformer-b0-finetuned-ade-512-512",
    "segformer.onnx",
    task="semantic-segmentation",
)

# Load and run inference
model = ONNXGeoModel("segformer.onnx", task="semantic-segmentation")
result = model.predict("input.tif", output_path="output.tif")
```

## Design Decisions

- ONNX/onnxruntime are **optional** dependencies — the package works without them
- Lazy imports throughout to avoid import-time errors
- Sidecar `.onnx.json` metadata file stores task type, id2label mapping, and input dimensions so `ONNXGeoModel` can auto-configure
- Uses `onnxruntime.get_available_providers()` to auto-select the best execution provider (CUDA if available, falls back to CPU)
- Pre-commit checks pass

pullRequest.title: Update QGIS plugin installation instructions
pullRequest.body: Make deepforest optional

pullRequest.title: fix: preserve class IDs in multi-class semantic segmentation masks
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes the bug where `SemanticSegmentationDataset` incorrectly binarized multi-class masks when a tile contained only two unique values.

## Problem

The previous logic converted masks like `{0, 3}` → `{0, 1}` when a tile happened to contain only two classes, losing the actual class information. This caused models trained with `num_classes > 2` to effectively learn a binary task instead of multi-class segmentation.

**Example:**
```python
label_mask = np.array([[0, 3, 3, 0]])
num_classes = 5

# Previous behavior (WRONG)
# Since len(unique_vals) == 2 and max > 1, it binarized:
(label_mask > 0).astype(np.int64)  # → [[0, 1, 1, 0]]  ❌ class 3 lost

# New behavior (CORRECT)
np.clip(label_mask, 0, num_classes - 1)  # → [[0, 3, 3, 0]]  ✅
```

## Solution

Restructured the mask normalization logic to:
1. **Binary segmentation (`num_classes == 2`)**: Normalize any non-zero value to foreground (1)
2. **Multi-class segmentation (`num_classes > 2`)**: Preserve class IDs and only clamp values to valid range `[0, num_classes-1]`

## Fixes

Closes #518

pullRequest.title: Fix SAM3 load in QGIS when pkg_resources is unavailable
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- fix QGIS SamGeo/SAM3 model loading in environments where `pkg_resources` is unavailable (e.g., newer setuptools setups on Windows/QGIS)
- add a lightweight runtime compatibility shim for `pkg_resources` used only when the module cannot be imported
- initialize this shim right before importing `samgeo` models in the SamGeo loader worker
- add tests for shim installation/no-op behavior

## Why
Users reported `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pkg_resources'` when loading SAM3 in geoai 0.29 under Windows/QGIS. This avoids requiring setuptools downgrade and removes hard runtime dependence on `pkg_resources` availability.

Closes #529

- ~500 lines modified/added

Fixes #531

pullRequest.title: Fix tiles parameter ignored in view_vector_interactive
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #563 where `tiles="Satellite"` (and other Google tile names) was silently ignored in `view_vector_interactive`
- The Google tiles branch resolved the tile name to a URL and stored it back in `kwargs`, but never called `m.add_tile_layer()` to actually add the basemap to the map
- Now explicitly adds the tile layer via `m.add_tile_layer()` and removes `tiles`/`attr` from `kwargs` so they don't leak into `add_gdf()`/`add_data()`
- Applies the same `kwargs` cleanup to the GeoTIFF (`.tif`) branch for consistency

## Test plan

- [ ] Test other Google tile names: `"Roadmap"`, `"Terrain"`, `"Hybrid"`
- [ ] Test with a local `.tif` file as the `tiles` parameter
- [ ] Test without any `tiles` parameter (default behavior unchanged)

pullRequest.title: Support GEOAI_CACHE_DIR env variable for custom install location
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Add `GEOAI_CACHE_DIR` environment variable (with `GEOAI_VENV_DIR` as fallback alias) to override the default `~/.qgis_geoai/` installation directory
- Add early writability check in `create_venv_and_install()` with actionable error message guiding users to the env variable
- Make the UI installation location label dynamic instead of hardcoded

## Motivation
Users in corporate environments with restricted permissions may not have write access to `~/.qgis_geoai/`, causing dependency installation to fail with "No pyvenv.cfg file" errors (closes #572).

## Changes
- **`venv_manager.py`**: `CACHE_DIR` reads from `GEOAI_CACHE_DIR` > `GEOAI_VENV_DIR` > `~/.qgis_geoai`. Added env var logging in `_log_system_info()` and writability check before downloads begin.
- **`python_manager.py`**: Same `CACHE_DIR` override for standalone Python directory.
- **`deps_install_dialog.py`**: Location label dynamically shows resolved `CACHE_DIR`.
- **`_geoai_lib.py`**: Diagnostic sys.path filter matches custom cache directories.
- **`docs/qgis_plugin.md`**: Documented `GEOAI_CACHE_DIR` with Linux/macOS/Windows examples.

## Usage
```bash
# Linux/macOS
export GEOAI_CACHE_DIR=/path/to/writable/directory

# Windows (Command Prompt)
set GEOAI_CACHE_DIR=D:\geoai_cache

# Windows (PowerShell)
$env:GEOAI_CACHE_DIR = "D:\geoai_cache"
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify default behavior unchanged (no env var set → uses `~/.qgis_geoai/`)
- [ ] Set `GEOAI_CACHE_DIR` to a writable directory → install succeeds at custom location
- [ ] Set `GEOAI_CACHE_DIR` to a non-writable path → clear error message displayed
- [ ] Verify UI label shows custom path when env var is set
- [ ] Verify `GEOAI_VENV_DIR` works as fallback alias

pullRequest.title: Add radiometric normalization (LIRRN) to landcover_utils
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Implements the LIRRN (Location-Independent Relative Radiometric Normalization) feature requested in #582. This adds a `normalize_radiometric()` function to `landcover_utils.py` that adjusts brightness and contrast of satellite images so they are comparable across different acquisition times, sensors, or atmospheric conditions.

**Reference:** doi:10.3390/s24072272

### Changes

- **`geoai/landcover_utils.py`**: Added `normalize_radiometric()` public API + 7 private helper functions (`_load_raster`, `_save_raster`, `_compute_distances`, `_compute_sample`, `_sample_selection`, `_linear_reg`, `_lirrn`)
- **`geoai/__init__.py`**: Registered `normalize_radiometric` in lazy symbol map
- **`tests/test_landcover_utils.py`**: New comprehensive test suite (62 tests)

### Key features

- Accepts file paths (GeoTIFF) or numpy arrays as input
- Preserves spatial metadata (CRS, transform) when saving output
- Returns per-band RMSE and adjusted R-squared quality metrics
- Uses modern `np.random.default_rng()` instead of global `np.random.seed()`
- Comprehensive input validation and edge-case handling

### Edge cases handled

- NaN/inf values in input (warns and replaces with 0)
- Constant-value or all-zero bands (warns and returns unchanged)
- Very small images where p_n exceeds pixel count
- Band count mismatches between subject and reference
- Insufficient regression samples

### Usage

```python
from geoai import normalize_radiometric

# File paths
norm_img, metrics = normalize_radiometric(
    "subject.tif", "reference.tif", output_path="normalized.tif"
)

# Numpy arrays
norm_img, metrics = normalize_radiometric(subject_array, reference_array)
print(f"RMSE per band: {metrics['rmse']}")
print(f"Adjusted R² per band: {metrics['r_adj']}")
```

## Test plan

- [x] 62 unit tests covering imports, signatures, validation, core array ops, edge cases, file I/O, and helper functions
- [x] All tests pass locally (`pytest tests/test_landcover_utils.py -v`)
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (black, codespell, trailing whitespace, EOF)
- [x] No new dependencies required (scikit-learn, scikit-image already in requirements.txt)

Closes #582

pullRequest.title: feat: Qt5/Qt6 dual compatibility for QGIS 4.0
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Migrates the GeoAI QGIS plugin to a single codebase that loads on **both** QGIS 3.28+ (Qt5/PyQt5) and QGIS 4.0 (Qt6/PyQt6) so the plugin lands on the official [QGIS 4 Ready list](https://plugins.qgis.org/plugins/new_qgis_ready/) without dropping existing 3.x users.

The migration was unusually clean: every import already routes through the `qgis.PyQt.*` abstraction, so no `if PYQT_VERSION_STR` branching was needed. Only 11 short-form enum / `exec_()` call sites needed qualification (PyQt5 >= 5.15 accepts the fully-qualified form, so the same code works on both Qt versions).

### Code changes (4 files, 11 sites)

- `geoai_plugin.py` - `dialog.exec_()` -> `dialog.exec()`
- `dialogs/update_checker.py` - 3x `Qt.AlignCenter` -> `Qt.AlignmentFlag.AlignCenter`; 6x `QMessageBox.Yes`/`QMessageBox.No` -> `QMessageBox.StandardButton.Yes`/`QMessageBox.StandardButton.No`
- `dialogs/instance_segmentation.py` - `Qt.Vertical` -> `Qt.Orientation.Vertical`
- `dialogs/samgeo.py` - `Qt.Checked` -> `Qt.CheckState.Checked.value`

### Metadata

- Add `qgisMaximumVersion=4.99` (the only declaration needed for the QGIS 4 Ready list; `supportsQt6=True` was removed in QGIS 4 core).
- Bump `version=1.1.0` -> `version=1.2.0` and prepend changelog entry.

### Tests, CI, security

- New `qgis_plugin/tests/` with a PyQt6 import-smoke suite that auto-discovers the plugin package and stubs `qgis.PyQt.*` onto real PyQt6 modules (mirroring the QGIS Qt6 shim's `QAction`/`QActionGroup`/`QShortcut` re-export from `QtGui`). 29 modules pass under PyQt6 locally.
- New `.github/workflows/qgis-plugin.yml` with two jobs: Bandit security scan and PyQt6 import smoke matrix (Python 3.10/3.11/3.12/3.13 with the Ubuntu Qt6 runtime libs `libegl1 libgl1 libxkbcommon0 libdbus-1-3 libfontconfig1`).
- New Bandit pre-commit hook scoped to `qgis_plugin/geoai/`. Bandit gate set to `--severity-level medium` to mirror the actual upload-block threshold at plugins.qgis.org. Plugin currently has 0 medium/high findings; pre-existing low-severity findings (`B110` try/except/pass, `B603` subprocess) are out of scope for the migration.

References:
- https://github.com/qgis/QGIS/wiki/Plugin-migration-to-be-compatible-with-Qt5-and-Qt6
- https://plugins.qgis.org/docs/migrate-qgis4

## Test plan

Automated (run locally and in CI):
- [x] `grep -rn '\.exec_('` returns nothing
- [x] `grep -rn 'Qt\\.AlignCenter\\|Qt\\.Checked\\|Qt\\.Vertical'` returns nothing
- [x] `grep -rn 'QMessageBox\\.\\(Yes\\|No\\)' | grep -v StandardButton` returns nothing
- [x] `python -m compileall qgis_plugin/geoai` succeeds under the active PyQt5 env
- [x] `pytest qgis_plugin/tests -v` - 29 PyQt6 import-smoke tests pass
- [x] `bandit -r qgis_plugin/geoai --severity-level medium` - `No issues identified.`
- [x] `pre-commit run --all-files` - clean (now includes Bandit)

Manual UI checks the maintainer must run before merging:
- [ ] Install branch into QGIS 3.28+ (Qt5). Click every menu action: DeepForest, Water Segmentation, Moondream, SamGeo, Segmentation, Instance Segmentation, Update checker, Log viewer, Dependency checker. Verify each dock widget opens, the Instance Segmentation splitter is vertical, AlignCenter labels render centered, the Update checker's Yes/No prompts work, and the modal `exec()` flow opens and closes cleanly.
- [ ] Install branch into a QGIS 4.0 nightly (Qt6). Repeat the full walk-through. Pay attention to keyboard modifier handling and `QMessageBox` confirmations.

pullRequest.title: fix: increase Moondream timeouts and add heartbeat mechanism
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #608 where users get "Moondream worker did not respond within 300s" during analysis
- Increases timeout constants to match SamGeo/DeepForest patterns (init: 600->900s, load_image: 120->300s, request: 300->600s)
- Adds `max(timeout, 1800)` for detect/point operations which involve inference + GeoDataFrame creation + serialization
- Adds heartbeat mechanism: worker subprocess sends `{"type": "heartbeat"}` every 30s during long operations, client consumes them and resets its timeout window -- prevents false timeouts while still detecting truly stuck processes
- Includes subprocess stderr in timeout error messages for better diagnostics

## Test plan
- [ ] Run Moondream caption/detect/query/point on sample images -- should complete without timeout
- [ ] Verify heartbeat messages appear in QGIS message log during long inference operations
- [ ] Force a timeout scenario (e.g., kill worker process) -- should still detect failure and include stderr in error

pullRequest.title: fix: add Linux fallback to system Python when standalone download fails
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #625 where Linux users (QGIS 3.40+, 3.44+) get dependency installation errors when the standalone Python download fails
- Adds `_get_linux_system_python()` that discovers and validates system `python3` (version >= 3.9, `venv` module available)
- Updates `_get_system_python()` and `create_venv_and_install()` with Linux fallback paths, mirroring the existing Windows fallback pattern
- Provides actionable error messages (e.g., `sudo apt install python3-venv`) when no suitable Python is found

## Test plan
- [ ] On Linux, delete `~/.qgis_geoai/python_standalone/` and trigger "Install Dependencies" in QGIS plugin -- should fall back to system Python and succeed
- [ ] Verify Windows behavior is unchanged (no modified code paths)
- [ ] Test on a system without `python3-venv` installed -- should show helpful error message

pullRequest.title: Preserve class labels and instance attributes in instance segmentation inference
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Captures class labels (`output["labels"]`) from Mask R-CNN predictions in `instance_segmentation_inference_on_geotiff()`, which were previously discarded
- Switches to compact mask storage (window-sized masks + offset) before NMS, reducing memory usage by orders of magnitude for large images
- Uses class-aware `batched_nms` instead of plain `nms` for better multi-class handling
- Outputs a 2-band raster (band 1: class labels, band 2: instance IDs) instead of single-band instance-only mask
- Returns a detections list with `mask`, `score`, `box`, `label`, and `instance_id` for downstream use
- Adds `nms_threshold` parameter to `instance_segmentation` and `instance_segmentation_batch`
- Enhances `detections_to_geodataframe()` with:
  - `use_mask_geometry` option to vectorize instance masks into polygon geometries (via `rasterio.features.shapes`) instead of bounding boxes
  - `simplify_tolerance` for polygon simplification
  - `instance_id` column in output GeoDataFrame
  - Optional `label` key (defaults to 1 for single-class models)

Closes #643

## Test plan
- [ ] Run instance segmentation inference on a GeoTIFF with a trained multi-class model
- [ ] Verify output raster has 2 bands (class labels + instance IDs)
- [ ] Verify returned detections list contains `mask`, `score`, `box`, `label`, `instance_id`
- [ ] Test `detections_to_geodataframe(detections, geotiff_path)` produces GeoDataFrame with `instance_id`
- [ ] Test `detections_to_geodataframe(detections, geotiff_path, use_mask_geometry=True)` produces polygon geometries from masks
- [ ] Verify backward compatibility: single-class models still work (label defaults to 1)

pullRequest.title: Add RF-DETR support for object detection on geospatial imagery
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #668

- Adds `geoai/rfdetr.py` module wrapping [RF-DETR](https://github.com/roboflow/rf-detr) for geospatial object detection
- Tiled inference on GeoTIFF with sliding window, cross-tile NMS, and georeferenced GeoDataFrame output
- 11 model variants (5 detection: base/nano/small/medium/large + 6 segmentation)
- Training wrapper (`rfdetr_train`) supporting COCO and YOLO format datasets
- `prepare_nwpu_for_rfdetr()` to prepare NWPU-VHR-10 aerial dataset for RF-DETR training
- HuggingFace Hub integration (push/pull trained models)
- Batch detection across multiple GeoTIFFs
- Example notebook with full training + inference workflow
- 28 unit tests, API docs page

## New Functions

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `rfdetr_detect()` | Tiled detection on GeoTIFF with NMS and GeoDataFrame output |
| `rfdetr_detect_batch()` | Batch detection on multiple GeoTIFFs |
| `rfdetr_train()` | Train RF-DETR on COCO/YOLO datasets |
| `prepare_nwpu_for_rfdetr()` | Download and prepare NWPU-VHR-10 for training |
| `push_rfdetr_to_hub()` | Push trained model to HuggingFace Hub |
| `rfdetr_detect_from_hub()` | Download model from HF Hub and run detection |
| `list_rfdetr_models()` | List 11 available model variants |

## Test plan

- [x] 28 unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_rfdetr.py`)
- [x] Pre-commit checks pass
- [x] Tested training on NWPU-VHR-10 dataset (10 epochs)
- [x] Tested inference on `cars-7cm.tif` with fine-tuned model (421 vehicles detected)
- [x] Verified GeoDataFrame output with correct CRS and georeferenced coordinates

pullRequest.title: fix: validate training paths to prevent PermissionError crash
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #672 - Instance segmentation (and semantic segmentation) training crashes with `PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied` on the labels directory when starting from the Train tab.

**Root cause:** Zero input validation before `os.listdir()` calls on user-provided directory paths in `train_MaskRCNN_model()` and `train_segmentation_model()`. On Windows, transient file locks from antivirus/indexing services cause `PermissionError` on freshly-created directories, and the error is wrapped as a generic `RuntimeError` with no actionable guidance.

**Changes:**

- **Core validation** (`geoai/train.py`): Add `_validate_training_paths()` with format-aware checks (directory/COCO/YOLO) and `_check_readable()` with Windows retry logic for transient file locks. Called from both `train_MaskRCNN_model()` and `train_segmentation_model()`.
- **UI validation** (`instance_segmentation.py`, `segmentation.py`): Add path validation in `start_training()` before launching subprocess - shows clear `QMessageBox` errors instead of crashing.
- **Error propagation** (`geoai_task_subprocess.py`): Preserve `PermissionError`/`FileNotFoundError` types across subprocess boundary instead of wrapping everything as `RuntimeError`.
- **Tests**: 19 new tests covering all validation paths, retry logic, and Windows-specific error hints.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/test_training_validation.py -v` - 19/19 passed
- [x] `pytest tests/test_instance_segmentation.py -v` - 19/19 passed (no regressions)
- [ ] Manual verification on Windows: Create Data → Train should show clear dialog on path issues instead of crashing

pullRequest.title: fix: adaptive GPU defaults and OOM retry for DeepForest predict_tile
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #673 - DeepForest `predict_tile` runs out of GPU memory on <=8 GB GPUs because the defaults (`batch_size=4`, `dataloader_strategy="batch"`) are tuned for 16 GB+ cards.

- **Detect GPU VRAM** at runtime via `torch.cuda.get_device_properties` (with `nvidia-smi` fallback)
- **Adaptive defaults** based on VRAM tier:
  - <=8 GB → `batch_size=1`, `strategy=window`
  - 8–12 GB → `batch_size=2`, `strategy=single`
  - >=12 GB → `batch_size=4`, `strategy=batch` (unchanged)
- **OOM-aware retry**: on CUDA OOM, clears cache, halves batch, retries with `window` strategy; if still OOM, falls back to CPU
- **Config restoration**: model config is snapshot/restored after retries so subsequent predictions are unaffected
- **Improved tooltips**: VRAM-based recommendations shown in UI; detected VRAM displayed in tile settings group title

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify adaptive defaults apply correctly when switching to "Large Tile" mode
- [ ] Verify user manual changes to batch_size/strategy are not overridden
- [ ] Test OOM retry path (if GPU available) or verify logic via code review
- [ ] Confirm existing tests pass (`pytest` — 405 passed, no regressions)

pullRequest.title: fix: avoid shadowing module logger in train_image_classifier
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Rename local `CSVLogger` variable to `csv_logger` in `train_image_classifier` to stop shadowing the module-level `logger`, which caused `UnboundLocalError` on earlier `logger.info()` calls.

Fixes #684

## Test plan
- [ ] Run the Image Recognition example notebook end-to-end

pullRequest.title: fix: run SamGeo vector export in venv subprocess (#688)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Routes SamGeo Segment Anything vector output (simple, smooth, orthogonalize modes) through the managed venv subprocess via `run_geoai_task` instead of importing `geoai.utils` inside the QGIS process.
- Adds a `raster_to_vector` task to `geoai_task_worker.py` for vector_mode 0 alongside the existing `vectorize_mask` and `smooth_vector` tasks.

Fixes #688. On native Windows QGIS, the bundled numpy 1.26.4 is loaded before the plugin venv (numpy 2.3.5) is prepended to `sys.path`, so direct `geoai.utils` imports in the QGIS process fail with `No module named 'numpy.lib.array_utils'`. Raster output already ran in the SamGeo subprocess; this change brings the vector path onto the same subprocess pattern already used by `segmentation.py` and `instance_segmentation.py`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Native Windows QGIS: Segment Anything -> load `uc_berkeley.tif` -> Output Format = Vector (GeoJSON) -> Segment by Box -> vector output written without numpy error
- [ ] Repeat with vector_mode = 1 (orthogonalize) and vector_mode = 2 (smooth)
- [ ] Linux: exercise same three modes, confirm no regression

pullRequest.title: feat: add fine-tuning support for pretrained segmentation models
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #692

- Add `freeze_encoder` parameter to `train_segmentation_model()` to freeze encoder weights during fine-tuning, so only the decoder is trained (reduces overfitting on small datasets)
- Reorder optimizer creation to come after checkpoint loading and encoder freezing, ensuring only trainable parameters are optimized
- Add "Fine-tuning (Optional)" group box to the QGIS plugin Train tab with:
  - Checkpoint file browser for selecting a pretrained `.pth` model
  - "Resume training" checkbox to restore optimizer/scheduler state
  - "Freeze encoder" checkbox to train decoder only
- Add checkpoint path validation and fine-tuning info logging in the GUI

The backend `checkpoint_path` and `resume_training` parameters already existed but were not exposed in the GUI. This PR wires them through and adds the new `freeze_encoder` capability.

## Test plan

- [ ] Train a model from scratch (no checkpoint) to verify no regressions
- [ ] Fine-tune from a checkpoint with `freeze_encoder=True` and verify encoder parameters are frozen
- [ ] Resume training from a checkpoint with `resume_training=True` and verify epoch/optimizer state is restored
- [ ] Verify the QGIS plugin Train tab shows the new Fine-tuning group with working file browser
- [ ] Verify checkpoint path validation rejects non-existent files

pullRequest.title: fix: pad undersized edge tiles in sliding-window inference
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Closes #696

- Add zero-padding for undersized edge tiles in `semantic_inference_on_geotiff`, `inference_on_geotiff`, and `object_detection_inference_on_geotiff`
- Crop model output back to actual tile dimensions before accumulating into the output array
- Filter spurious object detections that fall in the zero-padded region

## Test plan

- [x] All 1166 existing tests pass
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass
- [ ] Manual test with a non-square Sentinel-2 clip where one dimension is smaller than `window_size`

pullRequest.title: fix: orient rasterio-loaded images upright in FTW/training-tile display
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- `display_ftw_samples` and the `show_axes=True` branch of `display_training_tiles` were calling `matplotlib.imshow` without an explicit `origin`. South-up GeoTIFFs (e.g. FTW Sentinel-2 chips, whose affine `e` term is positive) therefore rendered vertically flipped.
- For `display_ftw_samples` (FTW-specific, consistently south-up), pass `origin="lower"` directly — matching the reporter's suggested fix.
- For `display_training_tiles` (generic, used with both FTW chips and north-up tiles from `export_geotiff_tiles`), derive `origin` from `src.transform.e` so south-up rasters render upright without regressing the common north-up case.
- The `show_axes=False` branch already routes through `rasterio.plot.show`, which respects the affine transform — left untouched.

Closes #703

## Test plan
- [ ] `geoai.display_ftw_samples("ftw_data", country="luxembourg", num_samples=4)` from `docs/examples/field_boundary_detection.ipynb` shows Sentinel-2 imagery and instance masks right-side up.
- [ ] `geoai.display_training_tiles(...)` against FTW tiles renders right-side up.
- [ ] `geoai.display_training_tiles(...)` against tiles from `geoai.export_geotiff_tiles` (north-up) still renders right-side up — no regression.
- [ ] `show_axes=False` path is visually unchanged.
- [ ] `pre-commit run --all-files` passes.

pullRequest.title: fix: qualify remaining short-form Qt enums for PyQt6 (#715)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes [#715](https://github.com/opengeos/geoai/issues/715), where the QGIS 4.0
plugin crashes with `AttributeError: type object 'Qt' has no attribute 'LeftDockWidgetArea'`
the moment a user clicks any tool button.

### Root cause

PR #707 (Qt5/Qt6 dual compatibility) qualified `AlignmentFlag`, `Orientation`,
`CheckState`, and `QMessageBox.StandardButton`, but missed four other enum
classes still referenced via short-form attributes:

| Short form | Qualified form | Sites |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Qt.LeftDockWidgetArea` / `Qt.RightDockWidgetArea` | `Qt.DockWidgetArea.<X>` | 14 |
| `Qt.ScrollBarAlwaysOff` | `Qt.ScrollBarPolicy.ScrollBarAlwaysOff` | 6 |
| `Qt.CrossCursor` | `Qt.CursorShape.CrossCursor` | 2 |
| `Qt.LeftButton` / `Qt.RightButton` | `Qt.MouseButton.<X>` | 5 |

These all live in method bodies (`__init__`, mouse event handlers), so the
PyQt6 import-smoke test added in #707 — which only catches class-body
regressions — passed cleanly while the plugin still crashed at runtime.

### Fix

Qualify all 27 occurrences across 9 files using the same pattern #707
established. PyQt5 5.15+ accepts the qualified form, so dual Qt5/Qt6
compatibility is preserved (no compat shim, no try/except imports).

Plugin version bumped to 1.2.1.

## Test plan

- [x] Static check: `grep -rE 'Qt\.(Left|Right|Top|Bottom)DockWidgetArea|Qt\.ScrollBarAlwaysOff|Qt\.CrossCursor|Qt\.(Left|Right|Mid)Button[^.]' qgis_plugin/geoai/` returns zero hits
- [x] PyQt6 import-smoke tests pass (29/29)
- [x] PyQt6 instantiation: `DepsInstallDockWidget()` constructs without `AttributeError` (the exact crash path from #715)
- [x] PyQt6 instantiation: `PointPromptTool` and `BoxPromptTool` construct without `AttributeError` (exercises `Qt.CursorShape.CrossCursor`)
- [x] All six new qualified enum members resolve under `PyQt6.QtCore.Qt`
- [x] `pre-commit run --all-files` passes (black, codespell, bandit, etc.)
- [ ] Issue #715 reporter to re-test on QGIS 4.0 / Windows once the next plugin release is available

## Follow-ups (not bundled here)

The import-smoke test could be extended to *instantiate* every dock widget,
which would catch this regression class. Worth a follow-up PR; kept out of
this one to keep it a focused bug fix.
